perturbation.original_text: Each party may use the information received from the other party hereunder and may provide such information to its parent corporation or any other subsidiaries ther eof and their respective employees as applicable for their use only in connection with the Business Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may use the data received from the other party hereunder and may provide such data to its parent corporation or any other subsidiaries ther eof and their respective employees as applicable for their use only in connection with the Business Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "information" to "data" without defining "data" creates confusion. The contract defines "the Information" later on; however, there is no clarity on whether "data" falls under the same definition, especially as it's used in the context of the Business Purpose here. This creates a contradiction, as some information is protected and some (data) might not be. This will affect section 1, the place of definition for 'the Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidentiality Each party may use the information received from the other party hereunder and may provide such information to its parent corporation or any other subsidiaries ther eof and their respective employees as applicable for their use only in connection with the Business Purpose. Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'information,' which is later defined as 'the Information' in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces 'information' with 'data.' Because 'data' is not explicitly defined or included within 'the Information' definition, it creates a contradiction. The contradicted text's confidentiality clause applies to 'the Information,' but it is unclear whether it also applies to 'data' after the change. This ambiguity introduces a contradiction, as some information is protected and some data might not be, thus breaking compatibility between the clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of documents from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'documents' creates a contradiction. The rest of the paragraph and section 1 still define what 'the Information' entails, which includes oral information and visual disclosures reduced to written form. 'Documents' is a narrower term, creating uncertainty about whether those other forms of communication are covered under the confidentiality obligations. The definition of 'the Information' itself becomes contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Confidentiality Each party may use the information received from the other party hereunder and may provide such information to its parent corporation or any other subsidiaries ther eof and their respective employees as applicable for their use only in connection with the Business Purpose. Each party agrees that, for a period of 5 years from receipt of information from the other party hereunder, such party shall use the same means it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propriet ary information to prevent the disclosure and to protect the confidentiality of both: i) written information received from the other party which is marked or identified as "confidential"; and ii) oral or visual information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure which is reduced to written form and provided to the other in such written form promptly after such oral or visual disclosure, hereinafter collectively referre d to as "the Information".
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'documents' in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text defines 'the Information' to include both written information and oral/visual information reduced to writing. By changing 'information' to 'documents,' the Changed Text narrows the scope of what is protected under the confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the original definition of 'the Information' as defined in the Contradicted Text because 'documents' is a subset of 'information', thereby making the obligation ambiguous and less inclusive.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of the Business Purpose and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of the Project and/or upon written request of the party providing the Data, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Business Purpose" to "Project" (inconsistently, as seen in the first perturbation) and "Information" to "Data" introduces ambiguity. The obligation is to return copies of 'the Information', not 'the Data'. This creates confusion: is there a separate obligation for Data? Are Project and Business Purpose the same? The section contradicts itself, as it mandates the return of 'the Information' while referring to the request for 'Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Return of Information Upon completion of the Business Purpose and/or upon written request of the party providing the Information, the other party shall within 14 days return all copies of th e Information to the providing party or certify in writing that all copies of the Information have been destroyed. Nothing in this clause shall affect the right of either party voluntarily to return the Information, or a ny part thereof, to the other party at any time.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Information' to 'Data' creates a contradiction. The contradicted text, and the changed text, both reference 'Return of Information'. However, the changed text now mandates returning 'the Information' after a request for 'Data'. This implies 'Data' is separate from 'Information', creating an inconsistency regarding what exactly needs to be returned, thus leading to an in-text contradiction. The explanation provided also points to this ambiguity, stating that the obligation is to return copies of 'the Information', not 'the Data'.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Materials.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Information' with 'Materials' without defining 'Materials' creates a contradiction within the section. The rest of the section refers to the 'Information', implying a different scope than 'Materials'. The definition of 'The Information' in section 1 is also affected because now what is warranted in section 3, is not the information, but 'the Materials'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. No Warranty/Liability Neither party makes any warranty, express or implied, with respect to the Information. Neither party shall be liable to the other or to any other person hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits or loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other or of such other person in connection with the provision or use of the Information hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text warranted 'Information', and the contradicted text also references the 'Information'. The changed text warrants 'Materials' instead of 'Information'. Because the contract defines the Information, the change creates a contradiction, as the warranty now applies to something that is not explicitly defined or consistently referenced, creating a misalignment as the definition of 'The Information' in section 1 is also affected because now what is warranted in section 3, is not the information, but 'the Materials'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Data is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" in the first part of the sentence while retaining "Information" later in the same sentence creates a clear contradiction. It's unclear whether injunctive relief applies to the disclosure of "Data" or only to "Information". The sentence initially refers to 'Data' as the subject of confidentiality, then uses 'the Information' to describe the harm resulting from its unauthorized use. This creates confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Injunctive Rights Each party acknowledges and agrees that the Information is the confidential and/or propri etary and/or trade secret information of the other and the unauthorised use or disc losure of the Information could cause irreparable harm and significant injury to the other party for which that other party would have no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, each party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights it may have at law or in equity, to seek and obtain immediate injunctive relief in respect of any breach or potential breach of this Agreement by the other.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by replacing 'Information' with 'Data' at the beginning of the sentence, while keeping 'Information' later in the same sentence. This creates ambiguity about whether the injunctive relief applies to 'Data', 'Information', or both. The contradicted text, which is the same as the original, refers only to 'Information'. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction because it's unclear if the protections outlined in the contradicted text now extend to 'Data' or if they remain limited to 'Information', as the contradicted text suggests.
